1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 082 — **two substantive prover lanes** building on the iter-081 in-flight unblocks; Lane 3 deferred pending Mathlib upstream).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill — C0 substantially complete with `instIsMonoidal_W` deferred, C1 scheduled iter-083+, C2 iter-084+, C3 iter-086+) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-081 ran two lanes; both made structural progress without regression but neither hit the closure target. Net 14 → 14. Highlights:
20	- **Lane 1 BasicOpenCech**: **S2+S3+S4 executed inline** atop iter-080's `letI` refactor. `have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n` plus 5-layer `dsimp` + full `simp [..., CochainComplex.of, ..., dif_pos hRel]` exposes the explicit alternating-sum form for both sides of the goal. Sorry shifted L1176 → L1196. **Key iter-081 correction**: the iter-080 recipe named `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` for S3, but that lemma does NOT exist in Mathlib — actual bridge is `dif_pos hRel` inside a *full* `simp` invocation.
21	- **Lane 2 Differentials**: `_root_.PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_comp` landed fully closed at L454–465 (third permitted helper). `h_zero` of `cotangentExactSeq_structure` successfully closed in-flight via Route (c) — chain preserved as body comment block at L496–525. **New blocker for `h_epi`**: typeclass-coercion mismatch — cannot synthesize `Module ↑(X.presheaf.obj U) ↑((relativeDifferentials f).val.obj U)` because the natural instance is over the `forget₂`-image ring. Per the iter-081 conditional clause, body left as single absorbed `sorry` at L526 to preserve 5-sorry budget.
22	
23	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
24	
25	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-082 plan |
26	|---|---|---|---|---|
27	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 1: complete Phase B (S5–S8 per-summand R-linearity) on top of iter-081's exposed alternating-sum form to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1196)** |
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 2: reinstate verified Route (c) chain for `h_zero` + introduce `exact_iff_stalkwise` second gap-fill (sorry body, 1-for-1 shift) + close `h_epi` via Route 2 (identify descent with `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map`)** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 1 | yes | C0 | off-limits (deferred pending Mathlib upstream — `stalk_tensorObj` for varying-ring R₀) |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-086+) |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
34	| **Total syntactic** | **14** | — | | |
35	
36	## Current Objectives
37	
38	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
39	
40	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — complete Phase B (S5–S8) to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
41	
42	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1196 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1241 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1270 `h_loc_exact`.
43	
44	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1196) by completing Phase B (per-summand R-linearity) on top of iter-081's exposed alternating-sum form. After iter-081's S2+S3+S4 inline advance, BOTH sides of the goal at the sorry have the form:
45	```
46	(piIsoPi Z₂).hom (eqToHom _ ∘ₗ
47	    (∑ i : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun j' =>
48	       Pi.π Z₁ (j' ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j').op)))
49	  ((piIsoPi Z₁).symm <arg>) j
50	```
51	where `<arg>` is `r • y` on the LHS and `y` on the RHS (with an outer `r • ·` at the j-component level).
52	
53	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** These iter-078/079/080/081 commits are essential prerequisites:
54	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 (iter-078).
55	- `intro r y; funext j` at L1102–1103 (iter-078).
56	- The named-per-i `letI` block at L920–949 (iter-080): `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i) := …; letI h_mod_pi_n : Module R (∀ i, Z_n i) := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3 — both `letI` (critical for typeclass visibility).
57	- **The iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain at L1120–1153 (NEW iter-081)**: `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`; then `have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n := by rcases n with _ | k · simp at hn · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]`; then `dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]`; then *full* `simp [FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor, .cosimplicialObjectFunctor, AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex, .obj, .objD, .map, CochainComplex.of, FormalCoproduct.evalOp, CosimplicialObject.δ, e₁, e₂, ModuleCat.piIsoPi, dif_pos hRel]`. This block is byte-for-byte equivalent to the iter-081 advance — **do NOT regress to an earlier shape**.
58	
59	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences. The R-action on `∀ i, Z_n i` is defined componentwise via `Pi.module`, where each `Z_n i = Γ(C.left, V_i)` is an R-algebra via the restriction map. After iter-081, the goal is in explicit alternating-sum form; the remaining Phase B work is per-summand R-linearity at fixed `i ∈ Fin (prev n + 2)`.
60	
61	**Recipe.**
62	
63	After the iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain, the goal exposes the alternating sum on both sides. Continue with:
64	
65	- **S5 — Surface per-`(i, j')` summands at the j-component.** Chain:
66	  - `Pi.π Z₁ k (e₁.symm z) = z k` from `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι`. Reduces `(piIsoPi Z₁).symm z (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)` to `z (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)`.
67	  - `eqToHom_app`, `LinearMap.comp_apply`, `Finset.sum_apply` to commute the `eqToHom` cast through the sum and apply pointwise.
68	  - `Pi.lift_apply` to expose the per-`j'` form.
69	  Result: each summand at fixed `i` and output index `j` is `(-1)^i • ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom (z (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` (LHS with `z = r • y`; RHS with `z = y` plus outer `r •`).
70	- **S6 — Distribute `r •` across RHS sum.** `Finset.smul_sum`; then `smul_comm` commutes the outer `r •` past `(-1)^i •` on each summand.
71	- **S7 — Per-summand R-linearity.** `apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro i _`.
72	  - LHS summand: `((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom ((r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))`.
73	    - `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) = (perI₁ (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)).smul r (y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` via `Pi.smul_apply` + the named-per-i `perI₁` from iter-080.
74	    - Unfold `perI₁`'s `RingHom.toModule.smul` to `(presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r * y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)`.
75	    - Apply `RingHom.map_mul` to split the `.hom` across the product: `((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom (a * b) = ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom a * ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom b`.
76	    - On `a = (presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r`: apply `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp` to collapse `presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op ∘ presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op = presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op`. Result: `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r`.
77	  - RHS summand: `r • ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom (y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` where the outer `r •` is `(perI₂ j).toSMul`, i.e., `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * _`.
78	  - Both sides now: `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom (y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))`.
79	- **S8** — close by `rfl` on each summand. The outer `Finset.sum_congr` then closes the whole goal.
80	
81	**Partial-credit fallback.** If S5 succeeds but S7 stalls on the `RingHom.map_mul` + `← presheaf.map_comp` step, leave the per-summand block as `?_` and document the per-summand goal in the body. This does NOT reduce the file sorry count but gives iter-083 a clean handoff. Do NOT stop early without documenting precisely what failed.
82	
83	**Approaches ruled out iter-076/078/079/080/081** (do NOT re-attempt):
84	- `rw [Pi.smul_apply]` against the anonymous-builder `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul` without the iter-080 refactor — "did not find the pattern".
85	- `letI` parallel instance to bypass `h_mod_pi₂` anonymity without making `h_mod_pi_n` itself a `letI`.
86	- `rfl` after S2's `dsimp` alone — sides are not defeq without the `(up ℕ).Rel` case-split + `dif_pos hRel` collapse.
87	- `exact map_smul (scK₀.f.hom) r _` — `scK₀.f` is k-linear, not R-linear (the R-linearity is the *construct*).
88	- `module` tactic — not a pure linear-combination shape; sides involve presheaf restriction maps.
89	- `simp only` (instead of full `simp`) for the S2+S3+S4 unfold — iter-081 confirmed fails.
90	- `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` — does NOT exist in Mathlib (iter-081 verified). Use `dif_pos hRel` inside full `simp` instead.
91	- `rw [dif_pos hRel]` after a `simp only` chain — the conditional `dite` isn't yet in the goal.
92	
93	**Hard constraints**:
94	- **Preserve the iter-080 `letI` refactor at L920–949 byte-for-byte** (both `letI perI_n` AND `letI h_mod_pi_n := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3).
95	- **Preserve `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418**.
96	- **Preserve iter-081's S2+S3+S4 chain at L1102–1153 byte-for-byte** (the `intro r y; funext j; simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` + `have hRel` + `dsimp only [...]` + full `simp [..., dif_pos hRel]` block). This is the iter-081 advance — regressing to an earlier shape forfeits the structural progress.
97	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11). The S5–S8 chain must run inline inside `h_diff_pi_smul_f`'s body. No extraction into thin helpers across iterations.
98	- **No new axioms.**
99	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies. `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (standard dev feedback loop). `lean_multi_attempt` for position-bound tactic-attempt previews is also allowed.
100	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`); hard cap **6** (no regression).
101	- The L502 / L826 / L854 sorries are dead-ends — out of scope.
102	- `g_R.map_smul'` (L1241) and `h_loc_exact` (L1270) are downstream — out of scope (gated on this lane).
103	
104	**Mathlib references confirmed iter-081** (verify via `lean_local_search`):
105	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` (S5 inverse projection).
106	- `Finset.sum_apply`, `Finset.smul_sum`, `Finset.sum_congr` (S5/S6/S7).

16	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes
17	
18	This file constructs the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}`
19	of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S`, together with its universal derivation
20	`d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. It also states the cotangent exact sequence and the
21	characterisation of smoothness in terms of local freeness of `Ω`.
22	
23	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
24	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
25	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 064 — scaffold)
28	
29	All main declarations have `sorry` bodies. Closure trajectory is estimated
30	at ~10 iterations per `STRATEGY.md`.
31	
32	## References
33	
34	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
35	-/
36	
37	set_option autoImplicit false
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
44	
45	variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}

47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/
48	
49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
63	
64	/-! ### Sheaf condition: decomposition
65	
66	Closing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` is the main outstanding
67	gap of Phase B step 1. The mathematical proof has three layers:
68	
69	1. **`KaehlerDifferential` commutes with localisation.** For a ring map
70	   `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Mathlib's
71	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (and the scheme variant
72	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`) provides this.
73	2. **The presheaf is a sheaf on the basis of affine basic opens.** On a
74	   basic open `D(f)` of `Spec B`, sections are `Ω_{B[1/f]/A}`. Gluing on a
75	   basic-open cover `{D(fᵢ)}` of `Spec B` follows from the localisation
76	   compatibility of step 1, applied to each `fᵢ`.
77	3. **Globalisation to all opens.** A presheaf on a scheme is a sheaf iff
78	   it is a sheaf on the basis of affine opens (more precisely, on the
79	   basic opens of every affine open). This is the standard scheme
80	   sheaf-of-modules construction.
81	
82	The substep theorems below are the natural decomposition. Each remains
83	`sorry` pending the full proof; the structure mirrors the blueprint at
84	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`. -/
85	
86	/-- Substep 1 (localisation compatibility): For an affine open `V` of `X`
87	mapped to an affine open `U` of `S`, the sections of
88	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` over `V` are isomorphic (as additive groups)
89	to the Kähler differential module `KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app (op V))`. -/
90	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
91	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
92	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
93	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
94	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
95	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
96	  rfl
97	
98	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials is a **sheaf** in the Zariski
99	topology: Kähler differentials commute with localisation, which gives the
100	gluing axiom on affine opens.
101	
102	**Status (iter-065):** Sorry. Proof requires combining:
103	- Substep 1 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler`, definitional)
104	- Substep 2: sheaf condition on the basis of basic opens, derived from
105	  `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule`
106	- Substep 3: globalisation from a basis to all opens (Mathlib has
107	  `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and `isSheafFor` lemmas
108	  for restricting to a basis)
109	
110	The route via `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` is not viable since that
111	requires the result we are trying to prove (the presheaf must already be
112	a sheaf to be packaged as a `SheafOfModules`). -/
113	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
114	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
115	      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by
116	  -- Strategy: reduce to the underlying presheaf of types via
117	  -- `isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp`, then verify the sheaf condition on
118	  -- affine opens using `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`.
119	  -- The proof requires substantial development of a
120	  -- "sheaf condition restricted to affine basis" lemma + the localisation
121	  -- compatibility for Kähler differentials in the scheme-language.
122	  sorry
123	
124	/-- The sheaf of relative differentials `Ω_{X/S}`.
125	
126	Packages the presheaf `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` with the sheaf axiom
127	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` into the standard `X.Modules` shape. -/
128	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
129	  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩
130	
131	/-! ## Universal derivation -/
132	
133	/-- The universal derivation `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`.
134	
135	On each affine open `V = Spec B` over `Spec A`, this is the Mathlib universal
136	derivation `d_{B/A} : B → Ω_{B/A}`. It is an `A`-linear sheaf map satisfying
137	the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a d(b) + b d(a)`. -/
138	noncomputable def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
139	    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
140	      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
141	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
142	    f.c
143	  let d' := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ'
144	  have h_eq : X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := rfl
145	  refine {
146	    app := fun U ↦ AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (d'.d (X := U)),
147	    naturality := fun U V g ↦ ?naturality
148	  }
149	  case naturality =>
150	    ext x
151	    simp only [sheafCompose_obj_obj, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj,
152	      CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj, Functor.comp_map, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
153	    suffices d'.d ((ConcreteCategory.hom (X.presheaf.map g)) x) =
154	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).map g)) (d'.d x) by
155	      simpa using this
156	    exact d'.d_map g x

158	/-! ## Cotangent exact sequence
159	
160	The cotangent exact sequence
161	```
162	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0
163	```
164	for a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S` is decomposed into five named sub-claims:
165	
166	1. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` — the base-change cotangent map
167	   `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`. Locally, this is
168	   `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`, where `A → A' → B` is the
169	   tower of structure ring maps on an affine chart.
170	2. `cotangentExactSeqBeta` — the relative-quotient cotangent map
171	   `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`. Locally, this is the surjection
172	   `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` induced by the inclusion `A → A'`
173	   that kills the `A`-linear derivations factoring through `A'`.
174	3. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha_comp_Beta` — composition zero `α ≫ β = 0`,
175	   from the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (the
176	   range of `mapBaseChange` lies in the kernel of `map`).
177	4. `cotangentExactSeqShortComplexExact` — exactness `ker β = im α`,
178	   from the same ring-level theorem and gluing.
179	5. `cotangentExactSeqBetaEpi` — `β` is an epi, from
180	   `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` applied on each affine chart.
181	
182	The headline theorem `cotangent_exact_sequence` then assembles the
183	existential bundle from these five components without further sorries. -/
184

767	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
768	
769	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
770	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
771	```
772	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
773	```
774	Built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
775	sequence (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`) and glued via the
776	compatibility above.
777	
778	The three sub-declarations `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`,
779	and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` carry the remaining sorries; this theorem
780	itself is closed by assembling them.
781	
782	The composition-zero proof obligation is now an explicit existential
783	witness rather than an in-type `by sorry`, eliminating the iter-064/065
784	nested `by sorry` placeholder inside `ShortComplex.mk`. -/
785	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
786	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
787	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
788	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f)
789	      (h : α ≫ β = 0),
790	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
791	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β h) ∧
792	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
793	  obtain ⟨h, hex, hep⟩ := cotangentExactSeq_structure f g
794	  exact ⟨_, _, h, hex, hep⟩
795	
796	/-! ## Smoothness and local freeness of `Ω` -/
797	
798	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
799	being locally free of the given rank.
800	
801	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
802	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
803	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
804	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
805	    Smooth f ↔
806	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
807	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
808	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
809	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
810	  sorry
811	
812	/-! ## Cotangent space at a section -/
813	
814	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is smooth and `s : S ⟶ X` is a section of `f`, then the
815	cotangent space `s^* Ω_{X/S}` is a locally free `𝒪_S`-module of rank `n`.
816	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
817	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
818	`k`-vector space. -/
819	theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S)
820	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
821	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
822	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :
823	    ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
824	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
825	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)
826	      Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
827	  sorry

829	/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/
830	
831	/-- The `O_C(U)`-module structure on `M(U)` viewed as a `k`-module via
832	restriction of scalars along the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. -/
833	noncomputable abbrev moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj
834	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
835	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
836	    (M : C.left.Modules)
837	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{u} k :=
838	  (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
839	
840	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
841	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
842	restriction of scalars. -/
843	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
844	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
845	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
846	    (M : C.left.Modules)
847	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
848	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
849	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
850	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
851	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
852	  -- naturality.
853	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
854	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
855	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
856	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
857	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
858	  -- Now goal:
859	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
860	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
861	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
862	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
863	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
864	    ext x
865	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
866	  rw [hpres]
867	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
868	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)
869	  -- hnat has smul on the *target* of (M.val.map f) at index (kU r).
870	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
871	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).
872	  -- By algebraMap_naturality, (R.map f).hom (kU r) = (kV r), which is what we need.
873	  have halg : (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
874	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
875	      (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r := by
876	    rw [← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection, ← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
877	    exact toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r
878	  -- Convert hnat's target smul to the desired form via halg
879	  have htarget : ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars
880	        (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))).obj (M.val.obj V)).smul
881	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
882	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := by
883	    change (M.val.obj V).smul ((RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
884	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)) =
885	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r)
886	    rw [halg]
887	  rw [htarget] at hnat
888	  exact hnat

890	/-- The restriction map for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`, extracted as a separate
891	definition so the elaborator does not have to inline the smul-naturality proof
892	inside the main `Functor` definition. -/
893	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
894	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
895	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
896	    (M : C.left.Modules)
897	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
898	    moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U ⟶ moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V :=
899	  ModuleCat.homMk (M.val.presheaf.map f) (moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat C M f)
900	
901	@[simp] lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂
902	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
903	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
904	    (M : C.left.Modules)
905	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
906	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map
907	        (moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f) = M.val.presheaf.map f := by
908	  unfold moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
909	  exact ModuleCat.forget₂_map_homMk _ _
910	
911	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
912	scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`.
913	
914	On each open `U`, the sections are the `O_C(U)`-module `M(U)` viewed as a
915	`k`-module via the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. The restriction maps are `k`-linear
916	by the naturality of this algebra map. -/
917	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
918	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
919	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
920	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
921	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
922	  obj U := moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U
923	  map f := moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f
924	  map_id U := by
925	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
926	    rw [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
927	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_id U
928	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
929	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
930	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
931	    simp only [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂]
932	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp f g
933	
934	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
935	its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
936	`O_C`-module. -/
937	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
938	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
939	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
940	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
941	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
942	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
943	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
944	  convert M.isSheaf using 1
945	
946	/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
947	scalars along the structure morphism. -/
948	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
949	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
950	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
951	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
952	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
953	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
954	
955	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
956	
957	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
958	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
959	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
960	
961	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
962	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
963	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
964	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
965	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
966	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
967	      Module.rank k
968	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
969	  sorry

86	hα_fac : ∀ d_t b, ((isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_t).app U .hom ((derivation' φ_g').d b)
87	  = d_t.d b
88	```
89	to unfold the inner term to `d_target.d b`, then `dsimp` through the
90	`AddMonoidHom.mk'` wrapper to expose `(derivation' φ_fg').d ((f.c.app U).hom b)`,
91	then unfold `cotangentExactSeqBeta` and use the symmetric `hβ_fac` to reduce
92	further to `(derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b)`, which vanishes by
93	`Derivation'.d_app` after the adjunction-coherence `f.c = adj_f.unit ≫
94	pushforward.map φ_2'` (the canonical `hcoh_app` identity).
95	
96	#### Tactics tried (all fail)
97	
98	| Tactic | Failure mode |
99	|---|---|
100	| `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]` after the main simp | "made no progress" (lemmas detected as unused) |
101	| `rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp]` directly | "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern `ModuleCat.Hom.hom (?f ≫ ?g)`" |
102	| `change ... = 0` (with explicit `.hom.comp ... .hom`) | succeeds, splits LHS |
103	| Then `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` | succeeds, distributes the application |
104	| Then `rw [hα_fac _ b]` | "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern `(ModuleCat.Hom.hom (((isUniversal' φ_g').desc ?m).app U)) ((derivation' φ_g').d b)`" |
105	| `erw [hα_fac _ b]` (after the same setup) | (deterministic) timeout at `whnf`, 200000 heartbeats |
106	| `simp only [hα_fac]` | "made no progress" |
107	| `rw [show ... = ... from hα_fac _ b]` | "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" (same as plain `rw`) |
108	| `simp` (after `apply postcomp_injective`, no `ext`) | "made no progress" beyond the `(f ≫ g).base → f.base ≫ g.base` cosmetic |
109	| `simp [Universal.fac]` (after `apply postcomp_injective`, no `ext`) | reports `Universal.fac` as unused; goal unchanged |
110	| `simp` after `ext U b` + the standard simp set | makes partial progress but does not close; `ModuleCat.hom_comp` and `LinearMap.zero_apply` flagged as unused |
111	| `cat_disch` from the top of the body | "tactic 'aesop' failed, failed to prove the goal after exhaustive search" |
112	| `exact?` from the top of the body | "could not close the goal" |
113	
114	#### Diagnosis
115	
116	The fundamental issue is **metavariable-elaboration mismatch** when matching
117	`hα_fac _ b`'s pattern against the inline `d_target` term.  The pattern is
118	`(ModuleCat.Hom.hom (((isUniversal' φ_g').desc ?d_t).app U)) ((derivation' φ_g').d b)`
119	where `?d_t` is the metavariable to unify against the inline structure.
120	Lean's unifier appears unable to unify `?d_t` against the let-bound inline
121	Derivation' structure that emerged from `unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha`;
122	the `erw` timeout at `whnf` supports this — the unifier is trying to compute
123	the type-class instance for the inline structure against the abstract
124	metavariable's class, which loops or balloons.
125	
126	The `change`-based split, which uses Lean's transparency configuration
127	rather than the simp/rw matcher, succeeds because it directly equates two
128	defeq terms without unifying.  But the subsequent `rw [hα_fac _ b]` falls
129	back to the unifier, which then fails.
130	
131	### Iter-081 unblock recipes
132	
133	Three independent routes are open:
134	
135	#### (a) Bind `d_target` via `set` BEFORE `apply postcomp_injective`
136	
137	The pattern-matching failure is over an *inline* structure.  If we bind it
138	to a named handle via `set d_target := { d := fun {U} ↦ ... } with hd_target`
139	before invoking the universal property, the inner expression becomes
140	`(ModuleCat.Hom.hom (((isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target).app U)) ((derivation' φ_g').d b)`
141	with `d_target` a named local hypothesis.  The pattern of `hα_fac d_target b`
142	would then unify trivially.
143	
144	Challenge: the inline structure is buried inside `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s
145	body after `unfold`.  The `set` needs to be placed *after* the unfold + simp
146	but the elaborator needs to recognise the structure to bind to it.  May
147	require `with hd_target` and manual unfolding of `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` to
148	expose the structure explicitly.
149	
150	#### (b) Refactor `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` to name `d_target`
151	
152	`cotangentExactSeqAlpha` is a protected declaration.  Refactoring its body
153	to expose `d_target` as a top-level `noncomputable def` (or as a named `let`
154	that survives the unfold) requires user authorisation.  This is the
155	cleanest fix but cannot be done by the prover this iteration.
156	
157	#### (c) Introduce `Derivation.postcomp_comp` and skip `ext U b`
158	
159	The lemma
160	```
161	Derivation.postcomp_comp : d.postcomp (f ≫ g) = (d.postcomp f).postcomp g
162	```
163	is missing from Mathlib (verified via `grep` in
164	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean`).  Adding
165	it as a one-line gap-fill (proof: `Derivation.ext fun U b ↦ by simp
166	[postcomp_d_apply, comp_app, hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]`) would let us
167	collapse
168	```
169	(derivation' φ_g').postcomp (desc d_target ≫ (pushforward _).map β.val)
170	  = ((derivation' φ_g').postcomp (desc d_target)).postcomp ((pushforward _).map β.val)
171	  = d_target.postcomp ((pushforward _).map β.val)   -- by Universal.fac
172	```
173	directly, sidestepping `ext U b` entirely.  The remaining obligation
174	`d_target.postcomp ((pushforward _).map β.val) = 0` can then be handled by
175	Derivation extensionality with a much shorter chain, since `d_target.d` is
176	the *outer* identifier we can `rw` against.
177	
178	This route adds one Mathlib-shape helper (`postcomp_comp`), within the
179	"two project-local helpers" budget the iter-080 plan permits for this lane.
180	But it requires further plan-agent authorisation since the iter-080 plan
181	specifically named `exact_iff_stalkwise` as the second helper.
182	
183	### `h_exact` and `h_epi` — not attempted standalone
184	
185	Per the iter-080 conditional clause, since `h_zero` did not close, the
186	`exact_iff_stalkwise` gap-fill was NOT introduced (introducing it without

191

193	unblock recipes above lands `h_zero`.

14	## Helpers landed (this iteration)

16	### `_root_.PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_comp` — fully closed (L455–465)
17	
18	```
19	@[simp]
20	lemma _root_.PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_comp
21	    {C : Type*} [Category C] {D : Type*} [Category D]
22	    {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat}
23	    {M N P : _root_.PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
24	    {φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R}
25	    (d : M.Derivation φ) (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) :
26	    d.postcomp (f ≫ g) = (d.postcomp f).postcomp g := by
27	  ext X b
28	  simp only [_root_.PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply,
29	             _root_.PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
30	  rfl
31	```
32	
33	Two structural notes:
34	- Inside the namespace `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, `PresheafOfModules` shadows the
35	  Mathlib root. Use `_root_.PresheafOfModules` for cross-namespace lemmas.
36	- After `simp only [postcomp_d_apply, comp_app]` the residual goal is
37	  `(f.app X ≫ g.app X).hom (d.d b) = (g.app X).hom ((f.app X).hom (d.d b))`,
38	  which closes by `rfl` (composition in `ModuleCat` IS `LinearMap.comp` at the
39	  `.hom` level; `simp only` does not fire on `ModuleCat.hom_comp` here because
40	  the LHS does not present in the rewriter's expected shape, but `rfl` recovers).
41	
42	## `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L514–542)
43	
44	### Route (c) successful for `h_zero` — verified working
45	
46	The full chain that closes `h_zero` was implemented and verified compiling in this
47	iteration. It is preserved as a comment block in the body for iter-082+. Key steps:
48	
49	```
50	apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
51	rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
52	unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
53	simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
54	apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
55	change (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' _).desc _ ≫
56	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).map
57	      (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val = 0
58	apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' _).postcomp_injective
59	rw [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_comp]
60	simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.Universal.fac]
61	apply PresheafOfModules.Derivation.ext
62	ext U b
63	-- ...set φ_g'/φ_fg'/φ_2'/adj_f, build hcoh, hcoh_app, hd_app, hβ_fac...
64	simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply]
65	dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
66	unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta
67	change (((isUniversal' φ_fg').desc _).app
68	    (op ((Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
69	      ((derivation' φ_fg').d ((f.c.app U).hom b)) = _
70	rw [hβ_fac _ ((f.c.app U).hom b)]
71	rw [hd_app]
72	rfl
73	```
74	
75	Validation: this chain successfully closes the `h_zero` goal (`lean_diagnostic_messages`
76	reported no errors when only the `h_exact` and `h_epi` sub-claims remained pending).
77	
78	**Sidesteps achieved by Route (c):**
79	- The iter-080 inline-`d_target` matcher pathology (a metavariable unification
80	  failure when `rw [hα_fac _ b]` tried to unify the universal `d_t` with the inline
81	  `d_target` from `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s body) is bypassed entirely: we never
82	  need to rewrite through the inline `d_target`. Instead, `Derivation.postcomp_comp`
83	  collapses the composition before the inline structure is exposed, and `Universal.fac`
84	  then rewrites `(derivation' φ_g').postcomp (desc d_target) ↦ d_target` cleanly.
85	- The same `hβ_fac` trick (parametrizing over an abstract `d_t`) DOES work cleanly
86	  for the β-side, because at that point we are at the bottom of the descent chain
87	  and the inline `d1` from `cotangentExactSeqBeta` matches the universal `d_t`.
88	
89	### Blocker for `h_epi` (the immediate next step)
90	
91	After `apply SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`, the goal reduces (via
92	`PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective`) to: for each `U`, the map
93	`((cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val.app U).hom` is surjective. Unfolding β makes
94	this `(d1.app U.unop).desc.hom`, where `d1` is the inline φ_fg'-derivation valued
95	in `relativeDifferentials' φ_2'` with `d1.d = (derivation' φ_2').d`.
96	
97	The mathematical content: this descent sends `D b ↦ D' b` (universal element of
98	target). By `KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation`, `{D' b}` spans the codomain.
99	Linear extension gives surjectivity.
100	
101	**The blocker**: when attempting `Submodule.mem_top` / `Submodule.span_induction`
102	on `y : (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj U`, Lean fails to synthesize
103	`Module ↑(X.presheaf.obj U) ↑((relativeDifferentials f).val.obj U)`. The reason is
104	that the natural module instance on `(relativeDifferentials f).val.obj U` is over
105	`(X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj U` (the forget₂-image), not directly
106	over `X.presheaf.obj U`. The two are the same underlying ring but different
107	categorical types, and instance synthesis does not bridge them.
108	
109	Mitigation routes for iter-082+:
110	1. **Coerce via `change`**: write `change ∃ x, _ = (y : _root_.KaehlerDifferential A B)`
111	   and supply the `Algebra A B := (φ_2'.app U).hom.toAlgebra` `letI` — attempted
112	   this iteration; the `change` succeeds but the subsequent `Submodule.mem_top y`
113	   still fails because the type of `y` after `change` is the coerced form, but
114	   `Submodule.mem_top` needs the original ModuleCat-valued type with its instance.
115	2. **Identify the descent with `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map`**: build an
116	   explicit coherence square `fac : η.app U ≫ φ_2'.app U = φ_fg'.app U ≫ 𝟙`
117	   (using `hη` from inside `cotangentExactSeqBeta`'s body) and use Mathlib's
118	   `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map_d` + `KaehlerDifferential.ext` to show
119	   `(d1.app U.unop).desc = CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map fac`. Then
120	   `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective_of_surjective` (with `surjective_id`)
121	   closes surjectivity. This is the "structural" route — requires extracting `hη`
122	   from `cotangentExactSeqBeta`'s body or recomputing it inline.
123	3. **Direct construction of preimage via section**: build a section
124	   `g : KaehlerDifferential (φ_2'.app U) → KaehlerDifferential (φ_fg'.app U)`
125	   sending `D' b ↦ D b` and show `(d1.app U.unop).desc ∘ g = id`. The right
126	   inverse needs `KaehlerDifferential.lift` machinery (the universal property
127	   applied to the target's universal derivation viewed as a φ_fg'-derivation
128	   via the η-coherence). This is essentially Route 2 in disguise.
129	
130	### Blocker for `h_exact`
131	
132	Independent of `h_epi`: requires the `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` helper
133	(authorised by the plan to carry `sorry` body, 1-for-1 shift). This iteration
134	introduced and reverted it because `h_epi` could not close in tandem (would have
135	been a regression 5 → 6). Iter-082+ should reintroduce.

12	/-!
13	# The differentials of a morphism in the category of commutative rings
14	
15	In this file, given a morphism `f : A ⟶ B` in the category `CommRingCat`,
16	and `M : ModuleCat B`, we define the type `M.Derivation f` of
17	derivations with values in `M` relative to `f`.
18	We also construct the module of differentials
19	`CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential f : ModuleCat B` and the corresponding derivation.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	universe v u
26	
27	open CategoryTheory
28	
29	attribute [local instance] IsScalarTower.of_compHom SMulCommClass.of_commMonoid
30	
31	namespace ModuleCat
32	
33	variable {A B : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{v} B) (f : A ⟶ B)
34	
35	/-- The type of derivations with values in a `B`-module `M` relative
36	to a morphism `f : A ⟶ B` in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
37	def Derivation : Type _ :=
38	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
39	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
40	  _root_.Derivation A B M
41	
42	namespace Derivation
43	
44	variable {M f}
45	
46	/-- Constructor for `ModuleCat.Derivation`. -/
47	def mk (d : B → M) (d_add : ∀ (b b' : B), d (b + b') = d b + d b' := by simp)
48	    (d_mul : ∀ (b b' : B), d (b * b') = b • d b' + b' • d b := by simp)
49	    (d_map : ∀ (a : A), d (f a) = 0 := by simp) :
50	    M.Derivation f :=
51	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
52	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
53	  { toFun := d
54	    map_add' := d_add
55	    map_smul' := fun a b ↦ by
56	      dsimp
57	      rw [RingHom.smul_toAlgebra, d_mul, d_map, smul_zero, add_zero]
58	      rfl
59	    map_one_eq_zero' := by
60	      dsimp
61	      rw [← f.hom.map_one, d_map]
62	    leibniz' := d_mul }
63	
64	variable (D : M.Derivation f)
65	
66	/-- The underlying map `B → M` of a derivation `M.Derivation f` when `M : ModuleCat B`
67	and `f : A ⟶ B` is a morphism in `CommRingCat`. -/
68	def d (b : B) : M :=
69	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
70	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
71	  _root_.Derivation.toLinearMap D b
72	
73	@[simp]
74	lemma d_add (b b' : B) : D.d (b + b') = D.d b + D.d b' := by simp [d]
75	
76	@[simp]
77	lemma d_mul (b b' : B) : D.d (b * b') = b • D.d b' + b' • D.d b := by simp [d]
78	
79	@[simp]
80	lemma d_map (a : A) : D.d (f a) = 0 :=
81	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
82	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
83	  D.map_algebraMap a
84	
85	end Derivation
86	
87	end ModuleCat
88	
89	namespace CommRingCat
90	
91	variable {A B A' B' : CommRingCat.{u}} {f : A ⟶ B} {f' : A' ⟶ B'}
92	  {g : A ⟶ A'} {g' : B ⟶ B'} (fac : g ≫ f' = f ≫ g')
93	
94	variable (f) in
95	/-- The module of differentials of a morphism `f : A ⟶ B` in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
96	noncomputable def KaehlerDifferential : ModuleCat.{u} B :=
97	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
98	  ModuleCat.of B (_root_.KaehlerDifferential A B)
99	
100	namespace KaehlerDifferential
101	
102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
103	variable (f) in
104	/-- The (universal) derivation in `(KaehlerDifferential f).Derivation f`
105	when `f : A ⟶ B` is a morphism in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
106	noncomputable def D : (KaehlerDifferential f).Derivation f :=
107	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
108	  ModuleCat.Derivation.mk
109	    (fun b ↦ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D A B b) (by simp) (by simp)
110	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D A B).map_algebraMap
111	
112	/-- When `f : A ⟶ B` is a morphism in the category `CommRingCat`, this is the
113	differential map `B → KaehlerDifferential f`. -/
114	noncomputable abbrev d (b : B) : KaehlerDifferential f := (D f).d b
115	
116	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
117	@[ext]
118	lemma ext {M : ModuleCat B} {α β : KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ M}
119	    (h : ∀ (b : B), α (d b) = β (d b)) : α = β := by
120	  rw [← sub_eq_zero]
121	  have : ⊤ ≤ LinearMap.ker (α - β).hom := by
122	    rw [← KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation, Submodule.span_le]
123	    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
124	    rw [SetLike.mem_coe, LinearMap.mem_ker, ModuleCat.hom_sub, LinearMap.sub_apply, sub_eq_zero]
125	    apply h
126	  rw [top_le_iff, LinearMap.ker_eq_top] at this
127	  ext : 1
128	  exact this
129	
130	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
131	/-- The map `KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g').obj (KaehlerDifferential f')`
132	induced by a commutative square (given by an equality `g ≫ f' = f ≫ g'`)
133	in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
134	noncomputable def map :
135	    KaehlerDifferential f ⟶
136	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g'.hom).obj (KaehlerDifferential f') :=
137	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
138	  letI := f'.hom.toAlgebra
139	  letI := g.hom.toAlgebra
140	  letI := g'.hom.toAlgebra
141	  letI := (g ≫ f').hom.toAlgebra
142	  have : IsScalarTower A A' B' := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' rfl
143	  have := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' (congrArg Hom.hom fac)
144	  -- TODO: after https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/pull/19511 we need to hint `(Y := ...)`.
145	  -- This suggests `restrictScalars` needs to be redesigned.
146	  ModuleCat.ofHom (Y := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g'.hom).obj (KaehlerDifferential f'))
147	  { toFun := fun x ↦ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B' x
148	    map_add' := by simp
149	    map_smul' := by simp }
150	
151	@[simp]
152	lemma map_d (b : B) : map fac (d b) = d (g' b) := by
153	  algebraize [f.hom, f'.hom, g.hom, g'.hom, f'.hom.comp g.hom]
154	  have := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' (congrArg Hom.hom fac)
155	  exact _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D A A' B B' b
156	
157	end KaehlerDifferential
158	
159	end CommRingCat

161	namespace ModuleCat.Derivation
162	
163	variable {A B : CommRingCat.{u}} {f : A ⟶ B}
164	  {M : ModuleCat.{u} B} (D : M.Derivation f)
165	
166	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
167	/-- Given `f : A ⟶ B` a morphism in the category `CommRingCat`, `M : ModuleCat B`,
168	and `D : M.Derivation f`, this is the induced
169	morphism `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ M`. -/
170	noncomputable def desc : CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ M :=
171	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
172	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
173	  ofHom D.liftKaehlerDifferential
174	
175	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
176	@[simp]
177	lemma desc_d (b : B) : D.desc (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d b) = D.d b := by
178	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
179	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
180	  apply D.liftKaehlerDifferential_comp_D
181	
182	end ModuleCat.Derivation

47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/

49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'

64	/-! ### Sheaf condition: decomposition
65	
66	Closing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` is the main outstanding
67	gap of Phase B step 1. The mathematical proof has three layers:
68	
69	1. **`KaehlerDifferential` commutes with localisation.** For a ring map
70	   `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Mathlib's
71	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (and the scheme variant
72	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`) provides this.
73	2. **The presheaf is a sheaf on the basis of affine basic opens.** On a
74	   basic open `D(f)` of `Spec B`, sections are `Ω_{B[1/f]/A}`. Gluing on a
75	   basic-open cover `{D(fᵢ)}` of `Spec B` follows from the localisation
76	   compatibility of step 1, applied to each `fᵢ`.
77	3. **Globalisation to all opens.** A presheaf on a scheme is a sheaf iff
78	   it is a sheaf on the basis of affine opens (more precisely, on the
79	   basic opens of every affine open). This is the standard scheme
80	   sheaf-of-modules construction.
81	
82	The substep theorems below are the natural decomposition. Each remains

14	
15	In this file, given a morphism `f : A ⟶ B` in the category `CommRingCat`,
16	and `M : ModuleCat B`, we define the type `M.Derivation f` of

36	to a morphism `f : A ⟶ B` in the category `CommRingCat`. -/
37	def Derivation : Type _ :=
38	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
39	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
40	  _root_.Derivation A B M

48	    (d_mul : ∀ (b b' : B), d (b * b') = b • d b' + b' • d b := by simp)
49	    (d_map : ∀ (a : A), d (f a) = 0 := by simp) :
50	    M.Derivation f :=
51	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
52	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
53	  { toFun := d
54	    map_add' := d_add
55	    map_smul' := fun a b ↦ by
56	      dsimp
57	      rw [RingHom.smul_toAlgebra, d_mul, d_map, smul_zero, add_zero]
58	      rfl
59	    map_one_eq_zero' := by
60	      dsimp
61	      rw [← f.hom.map_one, d_map]
62	    leibniz' := d_mul }
63	
64	variable (D : M.Derivation f)
65	
66	/-- The underlying map `B → M` of a derivation `M.Derivation f` when `M : ModuleCat B`
67	and `f : A ⟶ B` is a morphism in `CommRingCat`. -/
68	def d (b : B) : M :=
69	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
70	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom
71	  _root_.Derivation.toLinearMap D b
72	
73	@[simp]
74	lemma d_add (b b' : B) : D.d (b + b') = D.d b + D.d b' := by simp [d]
75	
76	@[simp]
77	lemma d_mul (b b' : B) : D.d (b * b') = b • D.d b' + b' • D.d b := by simp [d]
78	
79	@[simp]
80	lemma d_map (a : A) : D.d (f a) = 0 :=
81	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra
82	  letI := Module.compHom M f.hom

85	end Derivation

92	  {g : A ⟶ A'} {g' : B ⟶ B'} (fac : g ≫ f' = f ≫ g')

107	  letI := f.hom.toAlgebra

109	    (fun b ↦ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D A B b) (by simp) (by simp)
110	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D A B).map_algebraMap
111	
112	/-- When `f : A ⟶ B` is a morphism in the category `CommRingCat`, this is the
113	differential map `B → KaehlerDifferential f`. -/
114	noncomputable abbrev d (b : B) : KaehlerDifferential f := (D f).d b

116	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
117	@[ext]
118	lemma ext {M : ModuleCat B} {α β : KaehlerDifferential f ⟶ M}

6	module

9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic

11	/-!
12	# The presheaf of differentials of a presheaf of modules
13	
14	In this file, we define the type `M.Derivation φ` of derivations
15	with values in a presheaf of `R`-modules `M` relative to
16	a morphism of `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` of presheaves of commutative rings
17	over categories `C` and `D` that are related by a functor `F : C ⥤ D`.
18	We formalize the notion of universal derivation.
19	
20	Geometrically, if `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes (or more generally
21	a morphism of commutative ringed spaces), we would like to apply
22	these definitions in the case where `F` is the pullback functor from
23	open subsets of `S` to open subsets of `X` and `φ` is the
24	morphism $O_S ⟶ f_* O_X$.
25	
26	In order to prove that there exists a universal derivation, the target
27	of which shall be called the presheaf of relative differentials of `φ`,
28	we first study the case where `F` is the identity functor.
29	In this case where we have a morphism of presheaves of commutative
30	rings `φ' : S' ⟶ R`, we construct a derivation
31	`DifferentialsConstruction.derivation'` which is universal.
32	Then, the general case (TODO) shall be obtained by observing that
33	derivations for `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` identify to derivations
34	for `S' ⟶ R` where `S'` is the pullback by `F` of the presheaf of
35	commutative rings `S` (the data is the same: it suffices
36	to show that the two vanishing conditions `d_app` are equivalent).
37	
38	-/
39	
40	@[expose] public section
41	
42	universe v u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
43	
44	open CategoryTheory
45	
46	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]

48	namespace PresheafOfModules
49	
50	variable {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {F : C ⥤ D} {S' R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

52	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) (φ' : S' ⟶ R)

54	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`,
55	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
56	@[ext]
57	structure Derivation where
58	  /-- the underlying additive map `R.obj X →+ M.obj X` of a derivation -/
59	  d {X : Dᵒᵖ} : R.obj X →+ M.obj X
60	  d_mul {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a b : R.obj X) : d (a * b) = a • d b + b • d a := by cat_disch
61	  d_map {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
62	    d (R.map f x) = M.map f (d x) := by cat_disch
63	  d_app {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj X) : d (φ.app X a) = 0 := by cat_disch
64	
65	namespace Derivation
66	
67	-- Note: `d_app` cannot be a simp lemma because `dsimp` would
68	-- simplify the composition of functors `R ⋙ forget₂ _ _`
69	attribute [simp] d_mul d_map
70	
71	variable {M N φ}
72	
73	lemma congr_d {d d' : M.Derivation φ} (h : d = d') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
74	    d.d b = d'.d b := by rw [h]
75	
76	variable (d : M.Derivation φ)
77	
78	@[simp] lemma d_one (X : Dᵒᵖ) : d.d (X := X) 1 = 0 := by
79	  simpa using d.d_mul (X := X) 1 1
80	
81	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
82	/-- The postcomposition of a derivation by a morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
83	@[simps! d_apply]
84	def postcomp (f : M ⟶ N) : N.Derivation φ where
85	  d := (f.app _).hom.toAddMonoidHom.comp d.d
86	  d_map {X Y} g x := by simpa using naturality_apply f g (d.d x)
87	  d_app {X} a := by
88	    dsimp
89	    erw [d_app]
90	    rw [map_zero]
91	
92	/-- The universal property that a derivation `d : M.Derivation φ` must
93	satisfy so that the presheaf of modules `M` can be considered as the presheaf of
94	(relative) differentials of a presheaf of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
95	structure Universal where
96	  /-- An absolute derivation of `M'` descends as a morphism `M ⟶ M'`. -/
97	  desc {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
98	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : M ⟶ M'
99	  fac {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
100	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : d.postcomp (desc d') = d' := by cat_disch
101	  postcomp_injective {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
102	    (φ φ' : M ⟶ M') (h : d.postcomp φ = d.postcomp φ') : φ = φ' := by cat_disch
103	
104	attribute [simp] Universal.fac
105	
106	instance : Subsingleton d.Universal where
107	  allEq h₁ h₂ := by
108	    suffices ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
109	      (d' : M'.Derivation φ), h₁.desc d' = h₂.desc d' by
110	        cases h₁
111	        cases h₂
112	        simp only [Universal.mk.injEq]
113	        ext : 2
114	        apply this
115	    intro M' d'
116	    apply h₁.postcomp_injective
117	    simp
118	
119	end Derivation

121	/-- The property that there exists a universal derivation for
122	a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
123	class HasDifferentials : Prop where
124	  exists_universal_derivation : ∃ (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
125	      (d : M.Derivation φ), Nonempty d.Universal
126	
127	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ R`,
128	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
129	abbrev Derivation' : Type _ := M.Derivation (F := 𝟭 D) φ'
130	
131	namespace Derivation'
132	
133	variable {M φ'}
134	
135	@[simp]
136	lemma d_app (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a : S'.obj X) :
137	    d.d (φ'.app X a) = 0 :=
138	  Derivation.d_app d _
139	
140	/-- The derivation relative to the morphism of commutative rings `φ'.app X` induced by
141	a derivation relative to a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings. -/
142	noncomputable def app (d : M.Derivation' φ') (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X) :=
143	  ModuleCat.Derivation.mk (fun b ↦ d.d b)
144	
145	@[simp]
146	lemma app_apply (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
147	    (d.app X).d b = d.d b := rfl
148	
149	section
150	
151	variable (d : ∀ (X : Dᵒᵖ), (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X))
152	
153	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
154	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ'`, this is the
155	in derivation `M.Derivation' φ'` that is given by a compatible family of derivations
156	with values in the modules `M.obj X` for all `X`. -/
157	def mk (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
158	    (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x)) : M.Derivation' φ' where
159	  d {X} := AddMonoidHom.mk' (d X).d (by simp)
160	
161	variable (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
162	      (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x))
163	
164	@[simp]
165	lemma mk_app (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (mk d d_map).app X = d X := rfl
166	
167	/-- Constructor for `Derivation.Universal` in the case `F` is the identity functor. -/
168	def Universal.mk {d : M.Derivation' φ'}
169	    (desc : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
170	      (_ : M'.Derivation' φ'), M ⟶ M')
171	    (fac : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
172	      (d' : M'.Derivation' φ'), d.postcomp (desc d') = d')
173	    (postcomp_injective : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
174	      (α β : M ⟶ M'), d.postcomp α = d.postcomp β → α = β) : d.Universal where
175	  desc := desc
176	  fac := fac
177	  postcomp_injective := postcomp_injective
178	
179	end
180	
181	end Derivation'

183	namespace DifferentialsConstruction

185	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
186	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials of a morphism of presheaves of
187	commutative rings. -/
188	@[simps -isSimp]
189	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials' :
190	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
191	  obj X := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X)
192	  -- Have to hint `g' := R.map f` below, or it gets unfolded weirdly.
193	  map f := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map (g' := R.map f) (φ'.naturality f)
194	  -- Without `dsimp`, `ext` doesn't pick up the right lemmas.
195	  map_id _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
196	  map_comp _ _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
197	
198	attribute [simp] relativeDifferentials'_obj
199	
200	@[simp]
201	lemma relativeDifferentials'_map_d {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
202	    DFunLike.coe (α := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X))
203	      (β := fun _ ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app Y))
204	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((relativeDifferentials' φ').map f))
205	        (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d x) =
206	        CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d (R.map f x) :=
207	  CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map_d (φ'.naturality f) _
208	
209	/-- The universal derivation. -/
210	noncomputable def derivation' : (relativeDifferentials' φ').Derivation' φ' :=
211	  Derivation'.mk (fun X ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D (φ'.app X))
212	    (fun _ _ f x ↦ (relativeDifferentials'_map_d φ' f x).symm)
213	
214	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
215	/-- The derivation `Derivation' φ'` is universal. -/
216	noncomputable def isUniversal' : (derivation' φ').Universal :=
217	  Derivation'.Universal.mk
218	    (fun {M'} d' ↦
219	      { app := fun X ↦ (d'.app X).desc
220	        naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext (fun b ↦ by
221	          dsimp
222	          rw [ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d, Derivation'.app_apply]
223	          erw [relativeDifferentials'_map_d φ' f]
224	          simp) })
225	    (fun {M'} d' ↦ by
226	      ext X b
227	      apply ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d)
228	    (fun {M} α β h ↦ by
229	      ext1 X
230	      exact CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext (Derivation.congr_d h))
231	
232	instance : HasDifferentials (F := 𝟭 D) φ' := ⟨_, _, ⟨isUniversal' φ'⟩⟩
233	
234	end DifferentialsConstruction
235	
236	end PresheafOfModules

      (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk
        (cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g) (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) h).Exact ∧
      CategoryTheory.Epi (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) := by
  refine ⟨?h_zero, ?h_exact, ?h_epi⟩
  case h_zero =>
    -- Iter-082 reinstated Route (c) chain (closed in iter-081 in-flight).
    apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
    rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
    simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext

        (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).map
          (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val = 0
    apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' _).postcomp_injective
    rw [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_comp]
    simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.Universal.fac]
    apply PresheafOfModules.Derivation.ext
    ext U b
    -- Reintroduce the named ring-presheaf morphisms inline (consistent with
    -- `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`/`cotangentExactSeqBeta` bodies).
    set φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c with hφ_g'
    set φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶
        X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c with hφ_fg'
    set φ_2' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv

    have hd_app : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_2').d
